detailed_units.title: Meeting the In-Laws
detailed_units.can_do: Introduce yourself respectfully to extended family members.

phrases.en: Thank you, aunt, we are companions.

sentences.war: Yana, hi Ana ngan hi Ben bisita ha balay ni lola.
sentences.en: Today, Ana and Ben are guests at grandmother's house.

sentences.war: Aadi hi tiyo, hi tiya, ngan hi bayaw ni Ana.
sentences.en: Here are Ana's uncle, aunt, and brother-in-law.

sentences.war: Malipayon an pamilya ni Ana. Buotan hi lola.
sentences.en: Ana's family is happy. Grandmother is kind.

sentences.war: Dayaw ni Ben an kaupod.
sentences.en: Ben praises the companion.

detailed_units.title: Everyday Objects in the Yard
detailed_units.can_do: Ask for common household items outside or in the kitchen.

phrases.en: Do you want the torch over there?
Here are the spoon and fork.
How many cooking pots are yours?
Thank you, I want the coffee.

story.title: An Balay ni Lolo ug Lola

sentences.war: Maupay nga aga, lolo. Hain an balde?
sentences.en: Good morning, grandfather. Where is the bucket?

sentences.war: Aada an balde, apo. Kinahanglan mo an silhig?
sentences.en: The bucket is over there, grandchild. Do you need the broom?

sentences.war: Oo, lolo. Gusto ko maghugas hin pinggan ngan baso.
sentences.en: Yes, grandfather. I want to wash plates and glasses.

sentences.war: Dayaw, apo! Pagkaon kamo han isda ngan kan-on.
sentences.en: Good job, grandchild! Eat the fish and rice.

questions.q: Ano an gusto buhaton han apo?

detailed_units.title: Where are You Going? (Kain ka?)
detailed_units.can_do: Answer the common neighborhood passing greeting.

new_grammar.point: Asking and answering 'Where are you going?' (Kain ka?)
new_grammar.explain_en: The question 'Kain ka?' uses 'kain' for destination. Answers often use 'pakadto' (going to) or 'tikang' (coming from) followed by 'ha' and the place.

examples.en: I am going to the market.
I am coming from the church.

new_vocab.note: Used for destination, often with movement verbs.
Often used as an imperative or in future tense constructions.

new_vocab.gloss: going to, on the way to
new_vocab.note: Indicates current movement towards a place.

example.en: I am coming from the market.

new_vocab.note: Indicates origin or point of departure.
Can function as both a verb and a noun.
A common place for buying food and goods.
To go back to a previous location or state.
Refers to the act of cleaning or the state of being clean.

phrases.en: I am going to the market.
I am coming from the church.
I will return home right away.
I am going to the school.
I need work (or: I need to work).

sentences.war: Maupay nga aga, Lolo! Kain ka?
sentences.en: Good morning, Grandpa! Where are you going?

sentences.war: Pakadto ako ha merkado. Mapalit ako isda ngan utan.
sentences.en: I am going to the market. I will buy fish and vegetables.

sentences.war: Tikang ha merkado, pakadto hiya ha tiangge. Nagpalit hiya tinapay.
sentences.en: From the market, he went to the small store. He bought bread.

sentences.war: Didto ha balay, kinahanglan niya pahulay dayon. Limpyo an balay ni Lola.
sentences.en: At home, he needed to rest right away. Lola's house is clean.

questions.q: Kain pakadto hi Lolo ha primero?
Ano an kinahanglan ni Lolo didto ha balay?

detailed_units.title: Talking About the Weather
detailed_units.can_do: Comment on the heat, rain, and wind with community members.

new_vocab.note: Can also mean empty/finished for liquids.

phrases.en: The weather is really hot now.
It's raining already. The yard is wet.
The wind is strong. It's really windy.
It's sunny now. Good for laundry.

phrases.war: Dako nga bagyo an pakadto Daram.
phrases.en: A big typhoon is coming to Daram.

phrases.war: Kinahanglan uga an balay tikang ha baha.
phrases.en: The house needs to be dry from the flood.

sentences.war: Maupay nga aga! Init an panahon yana.
sentences.en: Good morning! The weather is hot now.

sentences.war: Kaina, nag-uuran hin makusog. Basa an natad.
sentences.en: Earlier, it rained strong. The yard is wet.

sentences.war: Pero yana, masirak na. Maupay para laba.
sentences.en: But now, it's sunny already. Good for laundry.

detailed_units.title: Time of Day and Simple Tasks
detailed_units.theme: Daily routines and coordinating activities in a rural island setting.
detailed_units.can_do: Coordinate basic daily times for chores or casual meetings.

new_grammar.explain_en: 'Alas' is used before a number (from 'usa' to 'napulo', 'dose') to indicate a specific hour of the day. It functions like 'at [time] o'clock'.

examples.en: We will wake up at five o'clock.

new_vocab.note: Used before a number to tell time.
to meet up (root kita = see); distinct from the pronoun kita = we

phrases.en: I will wake up at six tomorrow.

phrases.war: Tapos na an trabaho ko yana.
Magkita kita ha merkado alas tres.
phrases.en: Let's meet at the market at three o'clock.

phrases.war: Urhi hiya bumalik tikang ha balay.
phrases.en: He returned late from the house.
How many hours will you stroll?

phrases.war: Sunod nga adlaw, sugod kita paglimpyo.

sentences.war: Alas singko, sayu bumangon hi Nanay. Nagluto hiya kape ngan kan-on para ha pamilya.
sentences.en: At five o'clock, Mom woke up early. She cooked coffee and rice for the family.

sentences.war: Pagkatapos, nagpasyada hiya ha natad. Limpyo an ira balay pirmi.
sentences.en: Afterwards, she strolled in the yard. Their house is always clean.

sentences.war: Buwas, magkita hira ni Tatay ha merkado para magpalit isda.
sentences.en: Tomorrow, she and Dad will meet at the market to buy fish.

questions.q: Hain magkita hi Nanay ngan hi Tatay buwas?

detailed_units.title: Phase 2 Review & Celebration
detailed_units.theme: A small neighborhood celebration with food and friends.

phrases.en: We are happy that you are a visitor.
The fish is hot and delicious.

phrases.war: Hatag ko ha imo an prutas.
phrases.en: I give the fruit to you.
It's raining now. Tomorrow it will be sunny.
I am happy there in Daram.

phrases.war: Hatag ko ha imo an regalo.
phrases.en: I give the gift to you.

sentences.war: Maupay nga kulop! Dayon ha balay ni Nanay.
sentences.en: Good afternoon! Come in to Nanay's house.

sentences.war: Hi Nanay ngan hi Lola nagluto hin kan-on ngan isda. An isda malinamnam!
sentences.en: Nanay and Lola cooked rice and fish. The fish is delicious!

sentences.war: Kami malipayon nga ikaw kaupod.
sentences.en: We are happy that you are a companion.

sentences.war: Gusto mo ba tubig? Gusto mo ba kape? Aadi an prutas.
sentences.en: Do you want water? Do you want coffee? Here is the fruit.

questions.q: Ano an ginluto ni Nanay ngan ni Lola?
